---
title: "Latent process options for the growth-rate model"
description: "Random walks, group random slopes, and ARIMA(p, d, q) residuals — the time-series structures available in the formula interface for the growth rate."
author: Epinowcast Team
output:
  bookdown::html_vignette2:
    fig_caption: yes
    code_folding: show
pkgdown:
  as_is: true
bibliography: library.bib
csl: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/citation-style-language/styles/master/apa-numeric-superscript-brackets.csl
link-citations: true
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Latent process options for the growth-rate model}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---



The expectation module's `r` formula controls the latent log growth rate.
Several options exist for giving it time-series structure; each is a different choice for how `r` is allowed to vary over time and across groups.

This vignette walks through five of them on the same small slice of the German COVID-19 hospitalisation data:

- **Random walk on weeks** via `rw(week)`. The growth rate has iid Gaussian increments per week; the cumulative sum of those increments is what enters the linear predictor.
- **Independent per-week-and-group random effects** via `(1 | week:.group)`. Each (week, group) combination has its own random level drawn from a shared Gaussian, with no time-correlation structure across weeks.
- **Day-of-week random effects** via `(1 | day_of_week)`. Each weekday draws its own offset from a shared Gaussian, capturing periodic within-week variation that repeats across weeks rather than drifting over time.
- **ARIMA(p, d, q) residuals** via `arima(day, age_group, p, d, q)`. The growth rate is built up through a parameter-dependent kernel applied to unit-normal shocks, supporting arbitrary autoregressive and moving-average orders and integration. When `by` is supplied each group has its own innovation series; the AR/MA parameters and latent standard deviation are currently shared across groups.
- **Pure moving-average residuals** via `ma(day, age_group, q = 1)`. A stationary short-memory variant of the same machinery: shocks influence the series for `q` steps and then drop out, with no autoregressive feedback.

The fits use `enw_pathfinder()` for fast variational inference so the vignette stays snappy at build time.
For a real analysis switch to NUTS via `enw_sample()`.

# Setup


``` r
library(epinowcast)
library(data.table)
#> Warning: package 'data.table' was built under R version 4.5.2
library(ggplot2) # nolint: unused_import_linter. Required by plot.epinowcast().
#> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2
library(knitr)
```


``` r
options(mc.cores = 2)
```

We work with three German age strata reported nationally over five weeks of reference dates ending 28 days before the latest available reports.
The same `pobs` is used for every fit so any difference between the resulting nowcasts is down to the latent-process choice rather than the data.


``` r
nat_germany_hosp <- germany_covid19_hosp[
  location == "DE" & age_group %in% c("00+", "00-04", "80+")
]

retro <- nat_germany_hosp |>
  enw_filter_report_dates(remove_days = 28) |>
  enw_filter_reference_dates(include_days = 35)

pobs <- suppressWarnings(
  enw_preprocess_data(retro, by = "age_group", max_delay = 14)
)
```

A common fit configuration is reused across the models.
`enw_pathfinder()` is a fast approximate sampler that suits feature tours and exploration; for production fits use `enw_sample()` instead.


``` r
fit <- enw_fit_opts(
  sampler = enw_pathfinder,
  num_paths = 4, single_path_draws = 50, draws = 200,
  refresh = 0, show_messages = FALSE
)
```

# Random walk on weeks

`rw(week)` adds one Gaussian increment per week; the cumulative sum of those increments is added to `r`.
Mathematically this is equivalent to a (correlated) Gaussian prior over the per-week growth rates with a single shared standard deviation.


``` r
nowcast_rw <- epinowcast(
  pobs,
  expectation = enw_expectation(r = ~ rw(week), data = pobs),
  obs = enw_obs(family = "negbin", data = pobs),
  fit = fit
)
#> ℹ Using model /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library/epinowcast/stan/epinowcast.stan.
#> ℹ Include is /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library/epinowcast/stan.
#> ! `enw_cache_location` is not set.
#> ℹ Using `tempdir()` at
#>   /var/folders/cg/c1zk6s7n061cj176r8lp7_900000gn/T//RtmpZB1k6b for the
#>   epinowcast model cache location.
#> ℹ Set a specific cache location using `enw_set_cache` to control Stan
#>   recompilation in this R session or across R sessions.
#> ℹ For example: `enw_set_cache(tools::R_user_dir(package = "epinowcast",
#>   "cache"), type = c('session', 'persistent'))`.
#> ℹ See `?enw_set_cache` for details.
#> Init values were only set for a subset of parameters. 
#> Missing init values for the following parameters:
#>  - chain 1: expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 2: expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 3: expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 4: expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#> 
#> To disable this message use options(cmdstanr_warn_inits = FALSE).
#> 
#> 
#> Chain 1 Warning: file '/var/folders/cg/c1zk6s7n061cj176r8lp7_900000gn/T/RtmpZB1k6b/init-287e3f155596_1.json' is being used to initialize all 4 chains!
```


``` r
plot(nowcast_rw)
```

![Nowcast under a random walk on weeks.](figures/latent-processes-rw-plot-1.png)

# Independent per-(week, group) random effects

`(1 | week:.group)` adds a separate random level for every (week, group) cell, drawn from a shared Gaussian.
Unlike the random walk, there is no time correlation across weeks; each week starts afresh.
This is the natural choice when weekly fluctuations look like noise rather than drift.


``` r
nowcast_re <- epinowcast(
  pobs,
  expectation = enw_expectation(
    r = ~ 1 + (1 | week:.group), data = pobs
  ),
  obs = enw_obs(family = "negbin", data = pobs),
  fit = fit
)
#> ℹ Using model /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library/epinowcast/stan/epinowcast.stan.
#> ℹ Include is /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library/epinowcast/stan.
#> ! `enw_cache_location` is not set.
#> ℹ Using `tempdir()` at
#>   /var/folders/cg/c1zk6s7n061cj176r8lp7_900000gn/T//RtmpZB1k6b for the
#>   epinowcast model cache location.
#> ℹ Set a specific cache location using `enw_set_cache` to control Stan
#>   recompilation in this R session or across R sessions.
#> ℹ For example: `enw_set_cache(tools::R_user_dir(package = "epinowcast",
#>   "cache"), type = c('session', 'persistent'))`.
#> ℹ See `?enw_set_cache` for details.
#> Init values were only set for a subset of parameters. 
#> Missing init values for the following parameters:
#>  - chain 1: expr_arima_z, expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expr_arima_sigma, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 2: expr_arima_z, expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expr_arima_sigma, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 3: expr_arima_z, expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expr_arima_sigma, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 4: expr_arima_z, expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expr_arima_sigma, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#> 
#> To disable this message use options(cmdstanr_warn_inits = FALSE).
#> 
#> 
#> Chain 1 Warning: file '/var/folders/cg/c1zk6s7n061cj176r8lp7_900000gn/T/RtmpZB1k6b/init-287e6798e38c_1.json' is being used to initialize all 4 chains!
```


``` r
plot(nowcast_re)
```

![Nowcast under independent per-(week, group) random effects.](figures/latent-processes-re-plot-1.png)

# Day-of-week random effects

`(1 | day_of_week)` gives every weekday its own offset drawn from a shared Gaussian.
Unlike the random walk or the per-week random effects, the offsets are *periodic*: the same Monday offset enters every Monday, the same Tuesday offset enters every Tuesday, and so on.
This is the natural choice when within-week reporting variation looks structural rather than drifting.
The shared Gaussian pools information across weekdays, which is preferable to seven fixed effects whenever the dataset is short.


``` r
nowcast_dow <- epinowcast(
  pobs,
  expectation = enw_expectation(
    r = ~ 1 + (1 | day_of_week), data = pobs
  ),
  obs = enw_obs(family = "negbin", data = pobs),
  fit = fit
)
#> ℹ Using model /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library/epinowcast/stan/epinowcast.stan.
#> ℹ Include is /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library/epinowcast/stan.
#> ! `enw_cache_location` is not set.
#> ℹ Using `tempdir()` at
#>   /var/folders/cg/c1zk6s7n061cj176r8lp7_900000gn/T//RtmpZB1k6b for the
#>   epinowcast model cache location.
#> ℹ Set a specific cache location using `enw_set_cache` to control Stan
#>   recompilation in this R session or across R sessions.
#> ℹ For example: `enw_set_cache(tools::R_user_dir(package = "epinowcast",
#>   "cache"), type = c('session', 'persistent'))`.
#> ℹ See `?enw_set_cache` for details.
#> Init values were only set for a subset of parameters. 
#> Missing init values for the following parameters:
#>  - chain 1: expr_arima_z, expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expr_arima_sigma, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 2: expr_arima_z, expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expr_arima_sigma, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 3: expr_arima_z, expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expr_arima_sigma, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 4: expr_arima_z, expr_arima_pacf, expr_arima_theta, expr_arima_sigma, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#> 
#> To disable this message use options(cmdstanr_warn_inits = FALSE).
#> 
#> 
#> Chain 1 Warning: file '/var/folders/cg/c1zk6s7n061cj176r8lp7_900000gn/T/RtmpZB1k6b/init-287e5f8e3918_1.json' is being used to initialize all 4 chains!
```


``` r
plot(nowcast_dow)
```

![Nowcast under day-of-week random effects on the growth rate.](figures/latent-processes-dow-plot-1.png)

# ARIMA(p, d, q) residuals

`arima(time, by, p, d, q)` adds a latent ARIMA(p, d, q) residual series to the linear predictor.
Differencing is applied via the cumulative-sum operator and the ARMA part via a parameter-dependent Toeplitz kernel built from the impulse response; both compose with a single matrix multiplication onto unit-normal shocks.
When `by` is supplied each group has its own column of unit-normal shocks; the AR/MA parameters and latent standard deviation are currently shared across groups.

`arima(time, d = 1, p = 0, q = 0)` recovers the same model as `rw(time)`; setting `p > 0` or `q > 0` adds autoregressive or moving-average structure on top.


``` r
nowcast_arima <- epinowcast(
  pobs,
  expectation = enw_expectation(
    r = ~ 1 + arima(day, age_group, p = 1, d = 1),
    data = pobs
  ),
  obs = enw_obs(family = "negbin", data = pobs),
  fit = fit
)
#> ℹ Using model /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library/epinowcast/stan/epinowcast.stan.
#> ℹ Include is /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library/epinowcast/stan.
#> ! `enw_cache_location` is not set.
#> ℹ Using `tempdir()` at
#>   /var/folders/cg/c1zk6s7n061cj176r8lp7_900000gn/T//RtmpZB1k6b for the
#>   epinowcast model cache location.
#> ℹ Set a specific cache location using `enw_set_cache` to control Stan
#>   recompilation in this R session or across R sessions.
#> ℹ For example: `enw_set_cache(tools::R_user_dir(package = "epinowcast",
#>   "cache"), type = c('session', 'persistent'))`.
#> ℹ See `?enw_set_cache` for details.
#> Init values were only set for a subset of parameters. 
#> Missing init values for the following parameters:
#>  - chain 1: expr_arima_theta, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 2: expr_arima_theta, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 3: expr_arima_theta, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 4: expr_arima_theta, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#> 
#> To disable this message use options(cmdstanr_warn_inits = FALSE).
#> 
#> 
#> Chain 1 Warning: file '/var/folders/cg/c1zk6s7n061cj176r8lp7_900000gn/T/RtmpZB1k6b/init-287e3eea2b96_1.json' is being used to initialize all 4 chains!
```


``` r
plot(nowcast_arima)
```

![Nowcast under an ARIMA(1, 1, 0) residual on day.](figures/latent-processes-arima-plot-1.png)

The ARIMA-specific posterior summaries:


``` r
arima_pars <- summary(nowcast_arima, type = "fit")[
  grepl("expr_arima_(pacf|sigma)", variable),
  .(variable, mean, q5, q95)
]
kable(arima_pars, digits = 3)
```



|variable            |  mean|    q5|   q95|
|:-------------------|-----:|-----:|-----:|
|expr_arima_pacf[1]  | 0.029| 0.029| 0.029|
|expr_arima_sigma[1] | 0.007| 0.007| 0.007|



# Pure moving-average residuals

`ma(time, by, q)` is the convenience alias for `arima(time, by, p = 0, d = 0, q = q)`.
It is stationary (no integration) and has only `q` steps of memory: a shock at day `t` influences the series only at days `t, t + 1, ..., t + q` and then drops out.
This is useful when you expect short-range correlated noise around a flat trend rather than persistent drift or AR feedback.


``` r
nowcast_ma <- epinowcast(
  pobs,
  expectation = enw_expectation(
    r = ~ 1 + ma(day, age_group, q = 1),
    data = pobs
  ),
  obs = enw_obs(family = "negbin", data = pobs),
  fit = fit
)
#> ℹ Using model /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library/epinowcast/stan/epinowcast.stan.
#> ℹ Include is /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library/epinowcast/stan.
#> ! `enw_cache_location` is not set.
#> ℹ Using `tempdir()` at
#>   /var/folders/cg/c1zk6s7n061cj176r8lp7_900000gn/T//RtmpZB1k6b for the
#>   epinowcast model cache location.
#> ℹ Set a specific cache location using `enw_set_cache` to control Stan
#>   recompilation in this R session or across R sessions.
#> ℹ For example: `enw_set_cache(tools::R_user_dir(package = "epinowcast",
#>   "cache"), type = c('session', 'persistent'))`.
#> ℹ See `?enw_set_cache` for details.
#> Init values were only set for a subset of parameters. 
#> Missing init values for the following parameters:
#>  - chain 1: expr_arima_pacf, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 2: expr_arima_pacf, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 3: expr_arima_pacf, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#>  - chain 4: expr_arima_pacf, expl_arima_z, expl_arima_pacf, expl_arima_theta, expl_arima_sigma, refp_arima_z, refp_arima_pacf, refp_arima_theta, refp_arima_sigma, refp_arima_sd_sigma, refnp_arima_z, refnp_arima_pacf, refnp_arima_theta, refnp_arima_sigma, rep_arima_z, rep_arima_pacf, rep_arima_theta, rep_arima_sigma, miss_arima_z, miss_arima_pacf, miss_arima_theta, miss_arima_sigma
#> 
#> To disable this message use options(cmdstanr_warn_inits = FALSE).
#> 
#> 
#> Chain 1 Warning: file '/var/folders/cg/c1zk6s7n061cj176r8lp7_900000gn/T/RtmpZB1k6b/init-287ec2bc1ce_1.json' is being used to initialize all 4 chains!
```


``` r
plot(nowcast_ma)
```

![Nowcast under an MA(1) residual on day.](figures/latent-processes-ma-plot-1.png)

# When to reach for which

| Latent option | Formula example | Useful when |
|---|---|---|
| Random walk | `rw(week)` | The growth rate evolves smoothly week-to-week with no preferred direction; the standard non-stationary smoother. |
| Independent random effects | `(1 \| week:.group)` | Weekly fluctuations look like noise around a stable mean rather than a drifting trend; no time correlation is imposed. |
| Day-of-week random effects | `(1 \| day_of_week)` | Within-week variation is periodic — the same weekday offsets recur — and you want pooled rather than fixed weekday effects. |
| ARIMA | `arima(day, by, p, d, q)` | You want explicit autocorrelated short-range structure on the residuals, e.g. `arima(p = 1, d = 0)` for stationary AR(1) deviations around a smooth trend, or `arima(p = 1, d = 1)` for AR(1) on the differences. |
| Moving average | `ma(day, by, q)` | Stationary short-memory noise with finite influence: shocks decay to zero after `q` steps. Pair with a smooth trend for drift-free residuals. |

These options can be combined within the same formula. A common pattern is to mix a smooth trend with periodic and residual structure, e.g. `~ 1 + (1 | day_of_week) + arima(day, p = 1, d = 1)` for a day-of-week random effect alongside an ARIMA(1, 1, 0) residual.
The `by` argument on `arima()`, the aliases `ar()` / `ma()` / `arma()`, and `rw()` lets each group draw its own innovation series; the AR/MA parameters and latent standard deviation are currently shared across groups (per-group parameters are a planned extension).
